login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Use your operator user ID and password.
login.credentialsRequired: User ID and password are required.
login.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.
login.changeSubtitle: This account must change its temporary password before continuing.
login.newPasswordRequired: New password and confirmation are required.

onboarding.title: Create the first admin
onboarding.subtitle: Enter the setup token printed by the server, then create the first superadmin.

permissionDenied.title: Permission denied
permissionDenied.body: This page is only available to superadmins.

confirm.cascadePreview: These resources will also be removed:

tokenReveal.title: API token issued
tokenReveal.description: Copy this API token now — it will not be shown again.

users.title: Users
users.emptyBody: Create your first user to get started.

userCreate.title: New user
userCreate.invalidId: ID must start with a lowercase letter and use a-z, 0-9, dash or underscore.
userCreate.idHint: Lowercase letters, digits, dash and underscore. Must start with a letter.
userCreate.initialPasswordHint: Optional. Leave blank to create the user without Web login password.
userCreate.forcePasswordChange: Require password change on first login
userCreate.initialQuotaPending: Will provision quota on {{client}} after user creation.

userDetail.deleteBody: User {{id}} will be removed along with the dependents listed below. This cannot be undone.
userDetail.rotateBody: A new API token will be issued and the current one will stop working.
userDetail.resetPasswordBody: Reset the Web login password for {{id}}. Active Web sessions are revoked.
userDetail.generateTemporary: Leave blank to generate a temporary password
userDetail.requirePasswordChange: Require password change on next login

rules.title: Rules
rules.emptyBody: Push your first rule to start forwarding traffic.

rulePush.title: Push rule
rulePush.healthCheckIntervalHelp: Optional. When set, the client opens a TCP-connect probe to each target on this cadence. Range: 1–3600 seconds.
rulePush.sniPatternHelp: Exact host or single-label wildcard. Leave blank for plain TCP forwarding. Available only for TCP single-port rules.
rulePush.rateLimitHelp: Optional per-rule QoS caps. Leave fields blank to leave that dimension uncapped.

rateLimitForm.title: Rate limit
rateLimitForm.help: Each cap is independently optional. Empty = uncapped on that dimension.
rateLimitForm.burstHelp: Bursts must be between rate / 100 and rate × 60.

ruleDetail.title: Rule
ruleDetail.multiTargetTooltip: Multi-target rule — priority-ordered failover with per-target health tracking.

clients.title: Clients
clients.name: Name
clients.editDialogBody: Update editable metadata for this client. The client name is fixed after provisioning.
clients.revokeConfirmBody: This immediately invalidates the client token and disconnects any running client process. Reissue the client if it should connect again.
clients.deleteConfirmBody: This removes the client from the audit table. The name can be reused for a fresh provision afterwards. This is irreversible.
clients.emptyBody: Create an enrollment command so a forwarder can connect.

clientProvision.title: Enroll client
clientProvision.addressHint: Public IP address or DNS name users connect to for this client's forwarding rules. Do not include a URL scheme or port.

guide.heading: Install {{name}}
guide.expired: This command has expired — create a new one.
guide.skipNote: Already installed on this host? Skip to step {{step}}.
guide.stepEnrollSystemd: Redeem the command, then place the bundle
guide.stepEnrollDocker: Enroll once into a host volume
guide.dockerNote: install.sh is host-only; in Docker both commands run as your host user so the 0600 bundle is writable and readable by one identity.

clientDetail.reenrollConfirmBody: The current token keeps working until the client redeems the new one-time command. Redemption rotates the token and disconnects the old live session.

ownerQuotas.help: Per-owner caps apply across all rules a user pushes to this client. Per-owner ceilings bind before per-rule caps.
ownerQuotas.errorEmpty: Set at least one cap, or remove the envelope instead.
ownerQuotas.emptyBody: When operators push rules to this client, the owners appear here so you can apply per-tenant caps. You can also use "Add owner cap" to seed one ahead of time.
ownerQuotas.addDialogBody: Enter the user ID. You can set a cap before the user pushes a rule to this client; once they do, the cap applies automatically.
ownerQuotas.movedHint: Owner quota editing has moved to the user detail page.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.emptyBody: Operator allow / deny events will appear here as soon as they happen.

settings.title: Settings
settings.advertisedDescription: host:port the client uses to reach the gRPC control plane. Leave empty to auto-derive from this page's host. On Railway/reverse-proxied setups set the TCP-proxy host:port and ensure the server certificate covers that host.

userQuota.sectionHelp: Each row authorises this user on a specific client with optional bandwidth/concurrency limits.
userQuota.tableHelp: Per-client access and quota for this user.
userQuota.addDialogBody: Choose a client and define the access range for this user.
userQuota.deleteBody: Revoke {{user}}'s access to {{client}}? Any rules they pushed to this client will also be cleared.

row.duplicateTooltip: Backend has additional grants for this user+client; editing will merge them.
row.duplicateBanner: Detected {{count}} extra legacy grant(s). Saving here will merge them into one.
row.readOnlyHint: Edit this entry from the user detail page.
row.staleAfterDeleteHint: Update partially applied: previous grants were removed but the new one failed to create. Please re-add the entry.

form.unlimitedHelp: Allow access without bandwidth or concurrency limits.

userQuota.redirectNotice: Grants are now managed under each user's profile.
userQuota.clearUsageConfirm: Zeroes the bytes used in the current period. Billing anchor and period boundaries are NOT reset.
userQuota.deleteQuotaConfirm: Removes the monthly traffic quota for {{client}}. Traffic for this entry becomes unmetered.
userQuota.monthlyBytesHelp: Total bytes allowed per billing period. Set to 0 to disable forwarding.

traffic.empty: No traffic samples in the selected range.
